verdict.reasoning: FixedMoonVault.withdrawAll() is protected by the noReentrant modifier, and it also follows checks-effects-interactions: it reads the user's balance, then calls moonToken.burnAccount(msg.sender) before sending Ether with msg.sender.call{value: balance}(""). The external Ether transfer occurs only after the token balance has been burned, so a reentrant callback cannot withdraw the same funds again. MoonToken.burnAccount() and mint() are onlyOwner functions and update shared token state before any Ether interaction, so there is no cross-contract reentrancy path here.
